items.docs: List repositories with metadata.

Create a repository. Once the repository is created, it will also be created on the Git server.
The repository name must be unique within your account.

Once created, you can then push your code to this repository.

The repo will be available at `git.freestyle.sh/{repo-id}`

items.docs: Create a commit from files with automatic tree building and branch updates. Supports text files (UTF-8), binary files (base64), file deletions, and optimistic concurrency control via expectedSha. If the target branch does not exist, it is created as an orphan branch with this commit as its root.

params.name: rev
params.description: The git revision (branch name, commit SHA, etc.). Defaults to HEAD.

params.name: recursive
params.description:
If true, directory entries include their descendants recursively.
Defaults to false (immediate children only).

params.description: The repository id

params.name: hash
params.description: The object's hash

items.docs: Get a blob from the Git database. The contents will always be base64 encoded.

params.name: repo
params.description: The repository id

params.name: branch
params.description: Branch name (defaults to HEAD)

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of commits to return (default: 50, max: 500)

params.name: offset
params.description: Number of commits to skip (default: 0)

params.name: order
params.description: Sort order: "desc" (newest first, default) or "asc" (oldest first)

params.name: since
params.description:
Start point (older commit) for filtering/pagination (commit SHA)
- When used alone with order=desc: Returns commits newer than this (exclusive). Use for "what's new since X?"
- When used alone with order=asc: Starts from this commit (inclusive). Use with next_commit for pagination.
- When used with `until`: Defines the base of a range (like git's A..B where this is A)

params.name: until
params.description:
End point (newer commit) for filtering/pagination (commit SHA)
- When used alone with order=desc: Starts from this commit (inclusive). Use with next_commit for pagination.
- When used alone with order=asc: Returns commits up to and including this commit.
- When used with `since`: Defines the head of a range (like git's A..B where this is B)

When both `since` and `until` are provided, `until` must be a descendant of `since`.

params.name: disableTotalCount
params.description:
If true, skip the exhaustive history walk used to compute `total`.
Defaults to false. When true, the `total` field is omitted from the
response. Use this for cursor-based pagination where `total` is unused.

items.docs:
List commits with flexible filtering and pagination. Supports:
- Cursor-based pagination (recommended): Use `next_commit` from response with `until` (desc order) or `since` (asc order)
- Commit ranges: Specify both `since` and `until` to get commits between them (like git's `since..until`)
- Order control: `order=desc` (newest first, default) or `order=asc` (oldest first)
- Legacy offset pagination: Use `offset` param (deprecated, incompatible with since/until)

Requires repository visibility to

info.name: List All Tags in a Repository

params.name: repo
params.description: The repository id

items.docs:
List all tags in the Git repository with full details. Returns both annotated tags (with tagger and message)
        and lightweight tags. For each tag, the `sha` field contains the tag object SHA (for annotated tags) or
        commit SHA (for lightweight tags), while `target.sha` always contains the commit SHA the tag points to.

params.name: repo
params.description: The repository id

params.name: tag
params.description: The tag's name

items.docs:
Get a tag by its name. This is the recommended endpoint for retrieving tag information.
        Works with both annotated tags (returns tagger, message, and target commit) and lightweight tags
        (returns only name and target commit). For annotated tags, the `sha` field contains the tag object's SHA,
        while `target.sha` contains the commit SHA. For lightweight tags, both fields contain the same commit SHA.

        **Note**: If you need to retrieve an annotated tag object by its SHA

info.name: Get an Annotated Tag Object by SHA (advanced)

params.name: repo
params.description: The repository id

params.name: hash
params.description: The object's hash

items.docs:
Low-level endpoint for retrieving annotated tag objects directly from the Git database by their SHA hash.
        This endpoint only works with annotated tags and requires the tag object's SHA, not the tag name.


        **Most users should use `/git/v1/repo/{repo}/git/refs/tags/{tag}` instead**, which works with both
        annotated and lightweight tags and accepts tag names rather than SHAs. This endpoint is primarily
        for advanced use cases that need direct access to git database ob

params.name: repo
params.description: The repository id

params.name: hash
params.description: The object's hash

items.docs: Get a tree from the Git database with its entries.

info.name: List Repair Jobs for a Repository

items.docs: Returns every repair job (pending, running, completed, failed, cancelled) currently in the git server's in-memory registry. Pod restarts on the git server clear the list.

params.name: depth
params.description:
How far back from each ref tip to walk. Tip is depth=1.
Omit to walk the entire history (slow on huge repos).

items.docs: Spawns a background task on the git server that walks every ref's reachable history, substitutes missing blobs/trees with empty stand-ins, and persistently updates each ref to point at the rewritten tip. Returns immediately with a job ID; poll GET /git/v1/repo/{repo}/repair/{jobId} for status. Subject to a per-account concurrency cap.

info.name: Get the Status of a Repair Job

params.name: repo
params.description: Repository ID

params.name: job_id
params.description: Repair job ID returned by POST

items.docs: Get the Status of a Repair Job

params.name: repo
params.description: Repository ID

params.name: job_id
params.description: Repair job ID returned by POST

items.docs: Sets the cancel flag on the repair job. The worker observes it at the next commit/tree boundary and exits with status=cancelled. Already-terminal jobs are returned unchanged.

params.name: repo

params.name: query
params.description: The text (or regex pattern) to search for in file contents.

params.name: rev
params.description: The git revision (branch name, commit SHA, etc.). Defaults to HEAD.

params.name: pathPattern
params.description: Optional glob pattern to filter included file paths (e.g. "*.rs", "src/**/*.ts").

params.name: excludePattern
params.description: Optional glob pattern to exclude file paths (e.g. "node_modules/**", "*.min.js").

params.name: maxResults
params.description: Maximum number of matching files to return. Defaults to 100, max 1000.

params.name: caseSensitive
params.description: Whether the query is a case-sensitive search. Defaults to false.

params.name: isRegex
params.description: Whether the query is a regex pattern. Defaults to false.

params.name: wholeWord
params.description: Whether to match whole words only. Defaults to false.

params.name: offset
params.description: Number of results to skip for pagination. Defaults to 0.
